mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.25%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.25% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS signal an intensifying inflationary environment under active monetary restriction, which erodes corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of joint-signal instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion with a tight labor market, month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) intensify wage-pressure fears and force the Federal Reserve to maintain higher interest rates for longer, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE is below 4.5%, month-over-month PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 20-day S&P returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1,200 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST falls below 1200, positive 5-day S&P returns occur in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede earnings downgrades and risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a 0.2pp increase in UNRATE over 3 months, positive 60-day S&P returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling retail sales (RSAFS) and rising unemployment (UNRATE) signal a joint collapse in consumer spending and labor market health that triggers rapid risk-off repositioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of falling RSAFS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue streams and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a quarter of negative GDPC1 growth, positive 60-day S&P returns occur in more than 50% of instances over a 5-year rolling window.
